featureName: BUG-021-009: seasonal volume significance must be LLM-driven, not a hardcoded YoY ratio threshold
reporter: owner directive (no const limits; LLM decides per situation)

executionHistory.summary: After BUG-021-008 made expertise LLM-driven, an audit of sibling hardcoded business thresholds found DetectSeasonalPatterns (R-508, monthly report) deciding seasonal significance with hardcoded ratios: ratio < 0.7 (volume_drop), ratio > 1.5 (volume_spike), and a topic_seasonal claim for any topic with >= 5 same-month captures. A second finding: the seasonal.analyze ML enrichment was DEAD due to a request/response key mismatch (Go sent current_month/data_days/local_patterns; Python read patterns/month; Python returned patterns; Go read observations) — inert since BUG-003. Both answer/serve the same domain question docs/smackerel.md §3.6 requires to be LLM-driven.

Single bugfix-fastlane scope using Option A (owner-agreed): extend the EXISTING seasonal.analyze ML/NATS path to judge significance rather than add a new agent.Bridge scenario (seasonal already has an ML touchpoint; the prior four conversions used agent.Bridge only because those producers had none). The Go core gathers raw signals; the ML sidecar judges; operational bounds to fail-loud SST. SCN-021-SEASONAL-01..02 authored.

internal/intelligence/monthly.go: added SeasonalConfig (operational bounds); reworked DetectSeasonalPatterns to gather raw signals (this-month vs last-year volume; topic candidates above the operational floor, capped) and delegate significance to seasonal.analyze; removed the 0.7/1.5 ratio decision and the topic_seasonal '>=5' claim; capped results at MaxObservations; removed the caller-side '>2' cap in GenerateMonthlyReport. ml/app/intelligence.py: reworked handle_seasonal_analyze to a coherent contract (input raw signals; output {observations:[...]}) and to JUDGE significance, with no-LLM => empty observations. Engine gained a seasonal field + SetSeasonalConfig. SST: intelligence.seasonal.* (smackerel.yaml + config.sh + internal/config/seasonal.go). Wiring: wireSeasonalConfig + main.go call.

config/seasonal_test.go: SST populate + fail-loud + range. ml/tests/test_intelligence_handlers.py::TestSeasonalAnalyze reworked: no-LLM => empty observations (no ratio fallback), no-signals => empty, response shape has observations key. go build ./... + vet green; Go intelligence/config/cmd-core green; full Python suite 487 passed, 2 skipped.

./smackerel.sh config generate resolves the new keys into dev.env (lines 188-191). Existing TestDetectSeasonalPatterns_NilPool still passes (nil pool errors first). bug003_test.go seasonalAnalyzeReply binding test unaffected (response struct unchanged). Full Python ML suite green.

One reworked detector + one reworked ML handler; the dead second enrichment attempt (which previously appended ML observations on TOP of the hardcoded Go patterns) collapses into a single coherent path. The caller-side cap is removed in favor of the detector's MaxObservations.

DetectSeasonalPatterns collects all signals before the NATS request; nil config / nil NATS / request error / unmarshal error / no-signals all degrade gracefully to an omitted seasonal section. The ML handler's no-LLM path returns empty observations rather than fabricating significance.

The seasonal signal queries are parameterized (EXTRACT(MONTH)=$1, HAVING COUNT(*) >= $2, LIMIT $3). The NATS payload carries only aggregate counts + topic names (no artifact bodies / ids). The ML prompt is built from those aggregates only. No framework files touched.

artifact-lint PASSED for the packet; config generate + build + vet + affected Go packages + full Python suite green; ./smackerel.sh lint (Go lint + Python ruff + web-validate) passed (LINT_RC=0). Evidence in report.md.

No hardcoded seasonal-significance ratio remains (grep of 'ratio < 0.7' / 'ratio > 1.5' in monthly.go = 0). No migration; generated env gitignored; no .github/bubbles changes; the seasonal.analyze NATS subject/response names are unchanged (only payload shapes made coherent); parent spec 021 + spec 006 planning artifacts untouched.

monthly.go, intelligence.py, smackerel.yaml, and the wiring document the LLM-driven significance judgment via the ML path and the operational/business boundary. The packet records continuation of BUG-021-008, the Option-A rationale (reuse the existing ML path), and the fixed dead request/response contract.

scopeProgress.title: LLM-driven seasonal significance via the ML path

lockdownState.reason: bugfix-fastlane LLM-driven rework; no convergence loop required

resolvedFindings.summary: DetectSeasonalPatterns decided seasonal significance with hardcoded volume-ratio thresholds (ratio < 0.7, ratio > 1.5) and a 'topic with >= 5 same-month captures = seasonal' claim, contradicting docs/smackerel.md §3.6 and the owner directive.
resolvedFindings.resolution: Moved the significance judgment to the existing seasonal.analyze ML path (Option A); DetectSeasonalPatterns now gathers raw signals only; operational bounds moved to fail-loud SST. No hardcoded ratio remains; no-LLM/no-config/no-NATS skip.
resolvedFindings.evidence: report.md ML Handler / SST loader / Audit Evidence; internal/intelligence/monthly.go; ml/app/intelligence.py; grep of 'ratio < 0.7' / 'ratio > 1.5' in monthly.go = 0.

resolvedFindings.summary: The seasonal.analyze ML enrichment was inert since BUG-003 due to a request/response key mismatch (Go sent current_month/data_days/local_patterns and read observations; Python read patterns/month and returned patterns).
resolvedFindings.resolution: Reworked both ends to a single coherent contract: request {current_month,data_days,this_month_count,last_year_same_month_count,topic_candidates}; response {observations:[{pattern,month,observation,actionable}]}.
resolvedFindings.evidence: report.md ML Handler Evidence; ml/app/intelligence.py handle_seasonal_analyze; internal/intelligence/monthly.go DetectSeasonalPatterns.
